remember: Students will be able to recall the Rutherford's nuclear model of the atom.
Students will be able to describe Thomson's model of the atom.
Students will be able to list the limitations of Thomson's model.
Students will be able to recall the postulates of Rutherford's nuclear model.
Students will be able to describe the Rutherford's nuclear model diagrammatically.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of electron shells and subshells.
Students will be able to describe the Aufbau principle and Pauli's exclusion principle.
Students will be able to explain the concept of electron configuration.
Students will be able to describe the periodic table as a tabulation of atomic properties.
Students will be able to explain the significance of atomic number and mass number.
apply: Students will be able to write the electronic configuration of an atom.
Students will be able to predict the number of valence electrons in an atom.
Students will be able to determine the group and period of an element in the periodic table.
Students will be able to explain the relationship between atomic radius and the number of electron shells.
Students will be able to apply the periodic trends to predict the properties of an element.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast the different models of the atom.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between electron configuration and the periodic table.
Students will be able to evaluate the factors that influence the atomic radius of an element.
Students will be able to analyze the periodic trends and their applications.
Students will be able to explain the significance of atomic number and mass number in the context of nuclear chemistry.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To describe the Rutherford's nuclear model of the atom.
To explain the concept of electron shells and subshells.
To describe the periodic table as a tabulation of atomic properties.
board_exam_objectives: To recall the Rutherford's nuclear model of the atom and its postulates.
To explain the concept of electron configuration and its significance.
To describe the periodic table and its applications.
To predict the number of valence electrons in an atom and determine the group and period of an element in the periodic table.
To apply the periodic trends to predict the properties of an element.
